candidates.name: D3 Force Simulation
candidates.problem_it_solves: Custom brain-zone coordinates, force links, and dynamic graph transitions.
candidates.fit_assessment: High fit for custom Sanctum brain metaphors; requires custom rendering logic.

candidates.name: Sigma.js
candidates.problem_it_solves: High-performance rendering for large network graphs using WebGL.
candidates.fit_assessment: Strong fit for future large-scale brain maps; heavier than current mini scope.

candidates.name: Cytoscape.js
candidates.problem_it_solves: Interactive graph analysis + visualization with rich topology operations.
candidates.fit_assessment: Strong for operator graph analysis; may be overkill for strict UI shell only.

candidates.name: xterm.js
candidates.problem_it_solves: Rich terminal emulation with better interaction fidelity than plain text cards.
candidates.fit_assessment: Good fit for LIVE panel upgrade; requires stream adapter integration.

candidates.name: Motion
candidates.problem_it_solves: Declarative JS animations with performance guidance and rAF-based loops.
candidates.fit_assessment: High fit for controlled neural pulse/reveal effects without large framework lock-in.

candidates.name: requestAnimationFrame pattern
candidates.problem_it_solves: Frame-synced UI animation and measurable smoothness probes.
candidates.fit_assessment: Immediate fit; already used in performance probe approach.

candidates.name: Lit
candidates.problem_it_solves: Reusable reactive web components without full SPA framework migration.
candidates.fit_assessment: Good migration bridge from current vanilla structure to componentized Sanctum modules.

candidates.name: Playwright visual comparisons
candidates.problem_it_solves: Screenshot baseline drift detection for Sanctum UI truth claims.
candidates.fit_assessment: High fit; immediate value for anti-fake-green enforcement.

candidates.name: Server-Sent Events (EventSource)
candidates.problem_it_solves: One-way live telemetry push from backend to Sanctum clients.
candidates.fit_assessment: High fit for low-complexity telemetry channels.

candidates.name: WebSocket API
candidates.problem_it_solves: Bidirectional low-latency event channel for future interactive control loops.
candidates.fit_assessment: Strong fit for future remote control scenarios; more operational overhead than SSE.

candidates.name: htmx
candidates.problem_it_solves: HTML-centric interactivity with less JavaScript boilerplate.
candidates.fit_assessment: Not ideal for graph-heavy animated brain shell needing custom canvas/SVG control.
